edge_hypothesis: Supertrend's best regime is RANGING (PF=1.611 at ADX>5, PF=2.062 for MACD ranging) — yet all existing Supertrend specs either use default ADX>20 or push toward HIGH ADX (>30, >35). No spec explicitly isolates the RANGING regime where Supertrend actually performs best. The hypothesis: in low-ADX ranging markets (ADX 5-15), Supertrend direction flips near range support/resistance boundaries. Most of these flips lead to mean-reversion moves within the range — they travel to the opposite range boundary before Supertrend flips again. With extreme R:R (7:1-9:1), the majority of these range trades will hit the stop (the range isn't wide enough for 7x ATR TP), BUT the occasional range breakout — where price breaks through a range boundary and trends — will hit the extreme TP. The time cap serves a critical function here: in ranging markets, trades that haven't moved decisively in 6-8 bars are trapped in the mid-range chop zone. Exiting at market after 6-8 bars saves most of the stop loss capital on these chop trades. The winners (range breakouts) hit TP within 3-5 bars because genuine breakouts accelerate rapidly. Net effect: (a) fewer full-stop losses (most exit at partial loss via time cap), (b) same winner count (breakouts are fast), (c) improved PF because loss side improves. This directly complements st7a3k5w (ADX>30/35 trending specialist) — if both specs produce PF > 1.2, the optimal portfolio is: ranging Supertrend (this spec) + trending Supertrend (st7a3k5w) = full regime coverage with Supertrend. Invalidation: if PF < 0.8 at low ADX, ranging Supertrend flips are pure noise — the edge is entirely in trending markets.

variants.name: supertrend_1h_range_timecap6_8to1
variants.description: Supertrend direction flip on ETH/1h with ADX>5 (minimum — accepts ranging markets), 8:1 R:R (2.0x ATR stop, 16.0x ATR TP), and 6-bar time cap (6 hours). ADX>5 is deliberately permissive — it passes almost all market conditions except dead-flat. The key insight: Supertrend ADX>5 produced PF=1.611 in ranging (the system's 2nd best regime PF). The 6-bar time cap kills mid-range chop trades that slowly bleed to the full 2.0x ATR stop. At 8:1 R:R on 1h, only genuine range breakouts reach TP. Expected: ~100 trades (similar to ADX>5 base), improved loss profile from time cap, PF potentially above 1.6 if time caps reduce average loss without cutting winners.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (Supertrend turns bullish — direction change).
ADX_14 > 5 (minimum activity filter — excludes dead-flat markets only).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (Supertrend turns bearish — direction change).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 6 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — kills mid-range chop).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 0.8, ranging Supertrend at 8:1 R:R is noise — try 6:1 R:R to test if edge exists at lower R:R
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 6 bars at market price.

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip — range boundary signal in low-ADX conditions
ADX_14 > 5 — permissive filter, isolates ranging regime by excluding dead-flat only
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R with 6-bar time cap

variants.name: supertrend_1h_range_timecap8_7to1
variants.description: Same ranging Supertrend on ETH/1h but with ADX>10 (slightly more selective — excludes dead and near-dead markets), 7:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 14.0x TP), and 8-bar time cap (8 hours). This variant tests whether a slightly higher ADX floor improves signal quality. ADX 10-20 is the classic ranging zone. At 7:1 R:R (matching the MACD champion), direct PF comparison against MACD 7:1 (PF=1.712) reveals whether Supertrend can match MACD's signal quality in ranging. The 8-bar cap gives 33% more development time than the 6-bar variant — useful for Supertrend which may have slightly slower signal development than MACD zero-cross.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (Supertrend turns bullish).
ADX_14 > 10 (moderate activity filter — excludes dead and near-dead markets).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (Supertrend turns bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 8 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF at ADX>10 < PF at ADX>5, the lowest-ADX signals have the MOST edge — pure ranging is the sweet spot
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 8 bars at market price.

variants.name: supertrend_4h_range_timecap5_9to1
variants.description: Ranging Supertrend on ETH/4h with ADX>5 (maximum range inclusion), 9:1 R:R (2.0x stop, 18.0x TP), and 5-bar time cap (20 hours — roughly one trading day). The 4h timeframe tests whether Supertrend ranging signals are cleaner on higher timeframes where range boundaries are more established. At 9:1 R:R, only multi-day trend initiations from range breakouts reach TP. The 5-bar (20 hour) time cap is aggressive for 4h — it demands the breakout develops within one trading day, which is appropriate for genuine breakouts. Expected: fewer trades (~40-60), higher signal quality per trade, PF potentially > 1.3 if 4h ranges produce cleaner breakout signals than 1h.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (Supertrend turns bullish).
ADX_14 > 5 (minimum activity filter — maximum range inclusion).
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (Supertrend turns bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (18.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 5 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (time-decay exit — one trading day on 4h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if 4h PF < 1h PF at same ADX level, ranging Supertrend works better on shorter timeframes where range flips are more frequent
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 18.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 5 bars at market price.
